question.title: Daily Sales with 3-Day Moving Average
question.desc: Calculate daily sales and the 3-day moving average of sales for each day in a given dataset.
question.setup: DROP TABLE IF EXISTS sales; CREATE TABLE sales (date DATE, amount DECIMAL(10, 2)); INSERT INTO sales (date, amount) VALUES ('2022-01-01', 100.00), ('2022-01-02', 120.00), ('2022-01-03', 110.00), ('2022-01-04', 130.00), ('2022-01-05', 140.00);
question.solution: SELECT date, amount, AVG(amount) OVER (ORDER BY date ROWS 2 PRECEDING) AS moving_average FROM sales;
question.tips: Use window functions to calculate the moving average.
hints: Consider using a window function to calculate the moving average.
The ROWS clause can be used to specify the window frame.
The AVG function can be used with the OVER clause to calculate the moving average.

tests.setup: DROP TABLE IF EXISTS sales; CREATE TABLE sales (date DATE, amount DECIMAL(10, 2)); INSERT INTO sales (date, amount) VALUES ('2022-02-01', 200.00), ('2022-02-02', 220.00), ('2022-02-03', 210.00), ('2022-02-04', 230.00), ('2022-02-05', 240.00);

DROP TABLE IF EXISTS sales; CREATE TABLE sales (date DATE, amount DECIMAL(10, 2)); INSERT INTO sales (date, amount) VALUES ('2022-03-01', 50.00), ('2022-03-02', 60.00), ('2022-03-03', 55.00), ('2022-03-04', 65.00), ('2022-03-05', 70.00);
